title: Classification of Offenses Committed by Workers
content:
The offenses committed by workers under the 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs are classified into two categories: SERIOUS and LESS SERIOUS offenses.

The penalties for these offenses are as follows:

| OFFENSES   | 1st OFFENSE                                                                                               | 2nd OFFENSE                                                                                              | 3rd OFFENSE                                                                         |
|------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Serious    | Suspension from participation in the overseas employment program for six months and one day to one (1) year | Permanent disqualification from participation in the overseas employment program                         |                                                                                     |
| Less Serious | Suspension from participation in the overseas employment program for two months to six months           | Suspension from participation in the overseas employment program for six months and one day to one (1) year | Permanent disqualification from participation in the overseas employment program    |

Refer to Table 2 for the detailed list of offenses and their corresponding penalties.
